    "summary": "Sentry exposes a documented REST API on `https://{region}.sentry.io/api/0/` with an OpenAPI spec, plus an OAuth-protected MCP server at `https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp` over streamable HTTP.",
    "description": "Sentry is an application monitoring and observability platform for tracking errors, performance, releases, and related operational data. It helps development teams inspect issues and system behavior across their applications.",
    "credentials": {
      "sentry_auth_token": {
        "type": "bearer",
        "label": "Sentry auth token",
        "generateUrl": "https://docs.sentry.io/api/guides/create-auth-token/",
        "setup": "Create an internal integration in [Sentry](https://sentry.io/) via **Settings → Custom Integrations** (or **Settings → Developer Settings** for integrations), choose the needed scopes, save, then copy the generated token from **Tokens**. For user-specific endpoints, create a personal token from **User settings → Personal Tokens** as described in [Authentication](https://docs.sentry.io/api/auth/) and [Tutorial: Create a Sentry Authentication Token](https://docs.sentry.io/api/guides/create-auth-token/).",
        "acquisition": "manual"
      },
      "sentry_oauth_app": {
        "type": "oauth2",
        "label": "Sentry OAuth app/client",
        "generateUrl": "https://docs.sentry.io/integrations/integration-platform/",
        "setup": "In [Sentry](https://sentry.io/), go to **Settings → Developer Settings** and create a **Public Integration** if you need third-party OAuth access for users, or an **Internal Integration** for org-only use. Configure redirect URI and scopes, then use the client ID at `https://sentry.io/oauth/authorize/` and exchange the code with your client secret at `https://sentry.io/oauth/token/` as documented in [Authentication](https://docs.sentry.io/api/auth/) and [Integration Platform](https://docs.sentry.io/integrations/integration-platform/).",
        "acquisition": "manual"
      }
    },

    "summary": "Sentry exposes a REST Web API on sentry.io region hosts, an OAuth-protected remote MCP server at mcp.sentry.dev, and the sentry-cli command-line tool.",
    "description": "Sentry is an application monitoring platform for tracking errors, performance, logs, replays, and related observability data. Teams use it to inspect issues in production, manage releases, and analyze application behavior.",
    "credentials": {
      "sentry_auth_token": {
        "type": "bearer",
        "label": "Sentry auth token (organization, internal integration, or personal token)",
        "generateUrl": "https://sentry.io/orgredirect/organizations/:orgslug/settings/auth-tokens/",
        "setup": "Create a token in Sentry's developer settings. The easiest path for CI/CLI use is an Organization Token at [Auth Tokens](https://sentry.io/orgredirect/organizations/:orgslug/settings/auth-tokens/) under **Settings → Developer Settings → Organization Tokens**. For full org-scoped API access, create an internal integration from [Integration Platform](https://docs.sentry.io/integrations/integration-platform/) under **Settings → Developer Settings** and use its token. For user-scoped API access, create a Personal Token from **User Settings → Personal Tokens** as described in [Auth Tokens](https://docs.sentry.io/account/auth-tokens/). Assign the scopes your workflow needs from [Permissions & Scopes](https://docs.sentry.io/api/permissions/)."
      },
      "sentry_oauth_app": {
        "type": "oauth2",
        "label": "Sentry OAuth app client credentials",
        "generateUrl": "https://docs.sentry.io/product/partnership-platform/oauth-integration/",
        "setup": "To let users connect their Sentry account to your app, have Sentry register your OAuth application as described in [OAuth Integration](https://docs.sentry.io/product/partnership-platform/oauth-integration/). Provide your client name, redirect URIs, allowed origins, homepage, privacy policy, terms, and required scopes. Then send users to `https://sentry.io/oauth/authorize/` and exchange the returned code at `https://sentry.io/oauth/token/` as documented in [Authentication](https://docs.sentry.io/api/auth/#oauth2)."
      }
    },
